results.editorial:
Mintygreen's Bandar Sri Damansara branch occupies a commercial block at Sphere Damansara, Jalan PJU 10/9 in Prima Damansara — a location that sits at the Selayang-KL border and draws families from both Kepong and the northern PJ corridor. Part of a chain founded in 2017 and now operating across seven sites in the Klang Valley and Penang, the centre markets itself as the first senior care operator in Malaysia to structurally integrate Traditional Chinese Medicine into its standard programme. It serves a range of dependency levels, from semi-ambulant residents who need some daily assistance through to fully bedridden patients requiring round-the-clock nursing.

The website lists a broad scope of clinical nursing care: NG tube feeding, catheter care, insulin management, and pressure ulcer prevention and management. Day care and short-term respite stays are available alongside long-term placements, and the facility specifically mentions post-stroke, post-cancer, and post-operative recovery tracks. TCM physicians provide on-site acupuncture and Tui Na as complementary therapies. A general practitioner visits bi-weekly, and the wider Mintygreen group holds an AgeCope certification (listed on the AgeCope registry under the Emerald Care Center entity). Visiting hours are listed as 10am to 6pm.

One gap to note: bed capacity is not publicly disclosed for the BSD branch specifically, and pricing is not published online — you will need to call to get a quotation. The facility is physically inside a commercial shopblock rather than a purpose-built building, which affects the feel of communal outdoor space; ask about the activity area and how often residents access it. Languages supported appear to be English and Mandarin, consistent with the chain's bilingual marketing; families who need Malay-medium communication should confirm staff language coverage before committing.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, retirement home, day care, palliative/hospice
facts.licence_number: AgeCope certified (Emerald Care Center Sdn Bhd)
facts.red_flags: Bed count and pricing not published; commercial shopblock premises — ask about outdoor/activity space

results.editorial:
JJ Blessing Care Centre (互爱安老疗养中心) operates a branch at 12 Jalan Prima 6/3 in Taman Puchong Prima, Puchong — part of a small multi-site operator whose other known locations include Taman OUG in Kuala Lumpur and a branch in Ipoh. The Puchong Prima address is a residential-zone shophouse cluster, conveniently placed in an established neighbourhood with good road access to the Puchong Perdana and IOI City areas. Google Maps lists it under aged care with a rating of 4.6 from 44 reviews, which points to a reasonably well-regarded day-to-day operation even if the centre maintains a low public profile.

Public information on specific services is sparse. The facility's name and multi-branch footprint suggest it targets primarily Chinese-speaking families — the Chinese name translates roughly as 'Mutual Love Elder Care and Nursing Centre' — though no detailed service menu is publicly available. Operating hours listed on directory sites run from approximately 10:30am to 6:30pm, which is consistent with a centre that offers day care as its primary service, though whether 24-hour residential nursing is also available is not confirmed in any source reviewed.

Families considering JJ Blessing Puchong should ask directly about what care levels are accepted (ambulant-only versus bedridden), whether there is overnight nursing coverage, and the ratio of trained nurses to caregivers. No pricing, bed count, or JKM licence number is publicly stated. Given the limited online footprint, a site visit before committing is especially advisable to assess actual clinical capacity and environment.

facts.care_types: Aged care (day care likely primary; 24-hour residential unconfirmed)
facts.red_flags: Very limited public information on services; 24-hour nursing unconfirmed; no pricing or licence published

results.editorial:
Genesis Life Care Kajang is the flagship centre of one of Malaysia's largest private long-term care operators, located at 41 Jalan Ria 1 in Kawasan Perindustrian Ria — an industrial park setting off the Cheras-Kajang Highway that trades scenic surroundings for straightforward vehicle access from Cheras, Bangi, and Kajang town. The centre opened in 2021 and holds JKM (Jabatan Kebajikan Masyarakat) registration and AgeCope certification, making it one of the more formally credentialled facilities in the Kajang corridor. With 120+ beds, it is substantially larger than the typical independent nursing home in the area.

The care menu is genuinely broad: 24/7 nursing, weekly on-site GP consultations, a psychologist-led cognitive stimulation programme for dementia residents, physiotherapy and occupational therapy for stroke rehabilitation, palliative care, post-operative recovery, and a senior day care option. Dementia care uses individualised plans tied to the resident's history and stage, with art therapy, music sessions, and gentle exercise forming the daily activity spine. An in-house kitchen operates under dietician supervision. The Google rating stands at 4.9 from 37 reviews, reflecting strong family satisfaction in the early years of operation.

Pricing is not published and described only as contact-based depending on dependency level and room type; families should request a written breakdown. The 120-bed scale means the centre can feel busier than smaller homes — ask about actual staff-to-resident ratios on night shift and weekends. No overnight specialist cover is described; the weekly doctor visit model is standard for the sector but worth confirming in writing if your family member has complex medical needs.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, dementia care, stroke rehab, palliative, day care
facts.red_flags: Pricing not published; large centre — confirm night-shift staffing ratios

results.editorial:
Convee Elderly Care Center is located at Jalan Bunga Tanjung 22, Taman Seraya, Ampang — a quiet, established residential neighbourhood in the Ampang-Kuala Lumpur fringe that the Google Maps listing attributes to Selangor. The centre holds a 4.8 rating from 37 reviews, an unusually high score that suggests consistently positive feedback from families using the service. Its listed care types span three distinct service lines: nursing home residential care, adult day care, and home health care — making it one of the few smaller operators in the area advertising all three under one roof.

The facility's website describes care packages ranging from active-senior day care through to 24-hour residential care for bedridden residents, with packages described as personalised to individual needs. Regular health checks by doctors and licensed medical practitioners are listed as part of the care programme. The centre is accessible by bus (route 23 is mentioned) and has external parking, with ramp access indicated — practical details that matter for families bringing in residents who use wheelchairs.

Detailed service specifications — bed count, pricing, staffing composition, JKM licence number — are not published publicly. The home health care offering is particularly worth probing: ask whether nurses are dispatched for wound care, IV administration, or purely companion-level assistance, as the scope varies widely between operators. Families who want day care only should clarify start and end times and whether transport is provided. The strong Google rating is encouraging but independent verification through a site visit remains the essential step before placing a family member.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, adult day care, home health care
facts.red_flags: No pricing, bed count or JKM number published; home care scope needs clarification

results.editorial:
Bait Al-Mawaddah is an elderly care complex established in 2014 by Lembaga Zakat Selangor (LZS) and Majlis Agama Islam Selangor (MAIS), built on three acres of waqf land in Kampung Jalan Kebun, Section 30, Shah Alam. It was formally inaugurated by the Sultan of Selangor in September 2016. The centre operates under an explicitly Islamic welfare mandate — its name means 'House of Compassion' — and is designed to shelter elderly Muslim asnaf (zakat-eligible poor) in Selangor who are abandoned or lack family support capable of caring for them. The construction cost RM3 million and the facility has been recognised with a Selangor State Outstanding Senior Care Centre Award from JKM.

The centre has 26 rooms accommodating up to 52 residents. Facilities on site include a clinic room, physiotherapy room, prayer hall, lecture hall, dining hall, and landscaped gardens. Programmes include morning exercise classes, handicraft sessions, cooking activities, and regular Islamic education — fardhu ain classes and religious lectures. The health programme integrates disease prevention, continuous monitoring, psychosocial support, and healthy lifestyle practices as a formal framework.

Admission eligibility is strict and non-negotiable: applicants must be Muslim, registered as fakir or miskin asnaf with LZS, aged 60 or above, and — importantly — must be physically capable of self-care at the point of entry. This means Bait Al-Mawaddah does not accept bedridden residents or those with significant nursing dependency at admission. There is no fee structure published because the facility is funded through zakat and waqf. Families with a non-Muslim relative or a Muslim relative who does not qualify as asnaf will need to look elsewhere; this centre is not open to the general public.

facts.licence_number: JKM recognised (Selangor Outstanding Senior Care Centre Award recipient)
facts.red_flags: Restricted admission: Muslim LZS-registered asnaf aged 60+, must be self-sufficient at entry; not open to general public

results.editorial:
Mega Senior Care Centre is located at 29 Jalan Pantai 9/7, Seksyen 9, 46000 Petaling Jaya — a shophouse unit in a quiet residential street in PJ's older, well-established Section 9 neighbourhood. Despite the Google Maps area tag of 'Sek 9', the address firmly places it in Petaling Jaya rather than Shah Alam. The centre holds a perfect 5-star Google rating from 35 reviews, a signal worth noting — though a small review base means individual experiences carry significant weight in that average. The website positions the centre across three service categories: retirement living, dementia and palliative care, and home nursing.

The centre's website describes 24-hour care by certified caregivers, four daily home-cooked meals, senior-friendly facilities, and a daily activity programme of gentle exercise and interactive games. Cultural celebrations — Chinese New Year, Mother's Day — and intergenerational visits from local university students suggest an active social calendar. The dementia and palliative care streams appear to be genuine offerings rather than marketing labels, though the clinical depth of each is not independently documented beyond what the website states.

Key practical gaps: bed capacity, pricing, staffing qualifications, and JKM licence details are all absent from the public record. The website names individual staff members (Emie, Siri, Kay, Momo, Pearl) and visitor reviews speak warmly of their attentiveness — but families should ask for written confirmation of nurse-to-resident ratios, whether a GP visits on a fixed schedule, and what the escalation pathway is if a resident deteriorates overnight. The home nursing arm is worth enquiring about separately if your family member is not yet ready for full residential admission.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, dementia care, palliative care, home nursing
facts.red_flags: No bed count, pricing or JKM number published; small review base behind 5-star rating; night-shift clinical cover unconfirmed

results.editorial:
Pusat Jagaan dan Rawatan Orang Tua Al-Ikhlas is set inside a converted endowed mosque (masjid lama diwakafkan) at Lot 3530, Jalan Batu 18, Kampung Pulau Meranti, Puchong — a kampung enclave tucked between Puchong's commercial sprawl and the Putrajaya wetlands. The centre was founded in 1999 by Muji binti Hj Sulaiman, a former Chief Nurse at Hospital Serdang who brought home elderly patients discharged without family to receive them and has been running the home ever since. That clinical background — two decades of geriatric ward nursing before founding the home — is the most substantive credential in the public record. The facility currently cares for around 50 residents and employs approximately 12 staff.

The centre is Islamic in character, accepting donations of zakat and fitting its care philosophy within an Islamic welfare framework, though available sources do not state that admission is exclusively restricted to Muslims. Care provision includes daily personal assistance (bathing, dressing, feeding), basic medical monitoring, and social and recreational activities with a physical, mental, and spiritual dimension. Reviewer feedback on directory sites praises effective communication between staff and families and a holistic care approach.

The absence of any published pricing, bed availability, or formal JKM licence number means families must visit and ask directly. The converted mosque setting is unconventional and worth seeing in person — for some families, the quiet kampung environment and community character will feel exactly right; others may want a more medically equipped facility. Given the 50-resident scale and 12-person staff, confirm the nursing cover arrangement — specifically whether trained nurses or caregivers manage clinical tasks overnight, and what the protocol is for medical emergencies given the rural access point.

facts.religion: Islamic character; donations via zakat accepted
facts.licence_number: Registered with JKM (referenced in sources as government-registered since 1999)
facts.red_flags: No pricing, bed count published; remote kampung location — check emergency access and clinical staffing at night

results.editorial:
Sam Wound and Home Nursing Care is listed on Google Maps as a nursing agency and service establishment operating out of Selangor, with a contact number of +60 19-202 9418. The 5-star rating from 32 reviews is striking for any healthcare provider — and particularly so for a home nursing agency, where reviews typically reflect the personal relationship between nurse and family rather than institutional factors. As the name suggests, wound care appears to be the primary clinical offering, positioning the service for families managing post-operative wounds, diabetic ulcers, pressure injuries, or other complex wound dressings in a home setting.

Home nursing agencies of this type typically deploy registered or enrolled nurses to a patient's home for scheduled visits — usually one to three times per week for wound dressings — rather than providing continuous residential care. This is a fundamentally different service model from a residential nursing home: the patient stays at home, and the nurse comes to them. If correctly understood, Sam Wound and Home Nursing Care fills a specific and genuinely useful gap for families who want clinical nursing support without moving a family member into a facility.

No website, Facebook page, or formal business directory listing was found during research, which limits the ability to verify the scope of services, pricing, areas covered, or the qualifications of the nurses deployed. Families should ask directly about nurse registration status (Lembaga Jururawat Malaysia), the specific wound types handled, turnaround time for urgent callouts, and whether any non-wound nursing services (catheter care, NG tube, IV administration) are also available. A referral from a hospital discharge team or specialist nurse would add further confidence before engaging.

facts.care_types: Nursing agency, home health care (wound care primary)
facts.red_flags: No website or formal listing found; verify nurse registration (Lembaga Jururawat Malaysia) before engaging; service area and pricing unknown

results.editorial:
Elderlove Living Care Centre (Sg Long) is located at No. 11 Jalan Budiman, Budiman Business Park, Bandar Sungai Long — a light-industrial business park setting in the Kajang-Cheras corridor, accessible from the SILK Highway. The centre is the Sungai Long branch of a multi-site operator founded by Dr CS Lim, a consultant nephrologist, whose specialist background shaped the organisation's clinical positioning. Elderlove received the Malaysia Health and Wellness Brand Award in 2018 — cited as the first nursing home in Malaysia to do so — and runs five branches across the Klang Valley and Ipoh.

The service range is well-documented: 24/7 nursing with registered Malaysian nurses and SKM-3 certified caregivers, physiotherapy and rehabilitation, structured dementia care with trained caregivers and safe environmental adaptations, an AI Fitness Centre, and a day care programme running 9am to 6pm. An in-house specialist doctor conducts monthly reviews for residential patients; daily health monitoring is handled by nursing staff. Pricing is published on the website: long-term monthly rates run from RM3,500 (6-bedded room) to RM4,250 (2-bedded room); day care is RM125 per day for standard dependency, RM135 for higher dependency.

The business park location means there is no residential streetscape feel — outdoor space and natural light are worth asking about specifically, as they vary considerably between shopblock nursing homes. The monthly specialist review cycle (rather than weekly GP visits) suits stable long-term residents but may be insufficient for those with active medical complexity; confirm what the escalation pathway is for urgent clinical needs. Day care may suit families in the Kajang-Cheras corridor who want structured programming and nursing oversight without full residential placement.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, assisted living, day care
facts.licence_number: Malaysia Health and Wellness Brand Award 2018
facts.red_flags: Business park setting — ask about outdoor space; monthly (not weekly) specialist doctor review cycle; pricing published but confirm what is included

results.editorial:
Serene Skies Care Centre is described in its own website copy as a nursing home serving the Petaling Jaya area, with proximity to Subang Jaya, Puchong, and Kelana Jaya cited as part of its catchment. The Google Maps listing places it in Taman Seri Subang — a residential enclave adjacent to the Bandar Sunway township — which would make it genuinely accessible for families based anywhere in the Sunway-Subang-PJ triangle. The facility holds a perfect 5-star Google rating from 30 reviews, a score that reviewers attribute to attentive, compassionate staff.

The operator's website describes 24/7 care with a team of skilled nurses and caregivers, and explicitly lists dementia care (described as a memory care programme for Alzheimer's and other dementias), post-surgery recovery, and personalised medical support including medication management and physical therapy. Recreational activities and social events are noted as part of the daily programme. These are credible service claims for a residential nursing home in this price bracket and area, though no independent clinical accreditation or JKM licence number appears in the public record.

A significant limitation in researching this facility is that its official website is hosted on an Odoo platform and returned a 404 error during research, which makes it difficult to verify details such as bed count, room configurations, pricing, and staffing qualifications. Families should request a full written service schedule, pricing breakdown, and JKM registration details when making an enquiry. The Bandar Sunway location is commercially well-connected and near Sunway Medical Centre, which is a practical advantage if hospitalisation becomes necessary — ask whether the home has an existing referral relationship with nearby hospitals.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, dementia/memory care, post-surgery recovery
facts.red_flags: Website inaccessible during research; no JKM number or bed count in public record; pricing unknown — request written details before visiting
